relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The famine pressed harder.
The grain ran out, and Jacob told them to return.
Judah named the condition: Benjamin must come.
With Benjamin, they could go.
Without him, Joseph’s face would remain closed.
Israel felt harmed by the truth they had told.
They answered that the questions had drawn the family story out of them.
Judah took responsibility for life: send the boy, and we will live.
Judah became surety for Benjamin.
Delay had already cost them.
Israel prepared a gift from the land.
He sent double silver to repair the mystery.
Benjamin was finally released to go.
Jacob surrendered the matter to El Shaddai and to the risk of bereavement.
The brothers stood before Joseph with gift, silver, and Benjamin.
Joseph saw Benjamin and prepared a meal.
They were brought into Joseph’s house.
Fear interpreted hospitality as a trap.
They approached the steward at the entrance.
They explained the first journey.
They explained the returned silver.
They brought more silver, not knowing who had placed the first.
The steward spoke peace and brought Simeon out.
Water, foot washing, and feed replaced fear with welcome.
They prepared the gift for Joseph.
They bowed again as the dream continued unfolding.
Joseph asked after their father’s peace.
They answered and bowed again.
Joseph blessed Benjamin, his mother’s son.
Compassion surged, and Joseph hid himself to weep.
He washed his face and restrained himself for the meal.
Separation marked the table: Joseph, Hebrews, Egyptians.
The brothers sat in impossible birth order and were astonished.
Benjamin received a fivefold portion, and the table became merry.
